condition: PractitionerRole.practitioner references an existing Practitioner with a provider row
narrative_md: PractitionerRole enrichment of existing provider row. PractitionerRole does NOT create a new provider row; it adds specialty_concept_id, specialty_source_value, and care_site_id to the row created from the underlying Practitioner resource. One provider row per Practitioner; if multiple PractitionerRoles exist, choose the most recent or most specific.

fields.notes: SNOMED or NUCC specialty mapped to OMOP concept. Represents the most common or most specific specialty. 0 if unmapped. fhir-to-omop-demo extracts from PractitionerRole.specialty[0].coding[0] (lines 10-23, 44).

sources.comment: Extracts from specialty[0].coding[0] with multi-coding warning; merges into provider row

references.notes: specialty extraction lines 10-23; merge lines 31-53; result at line 44

sources.comment: Does not process PractitionerRole; specialty left unmapped

references.notes: Only Practitioner resource is mapped; no PractitionerRole handling

sources.comment: Falls back to Practitioner.qualification[0].code when no PractitionerRole

fields.notes: Raw specialty code as it appears in source data. Includes physician specialties (internal medicine, emergency medicine) and allied health professionals. fhir-to-omop-demo uses specialty.concept_code (line 49).

sources.comment: Uses specialty.concept_code after OMOP lookup (post-translation value)
Uses qualification[0].code.coding[0].code from Practitioner (no PractitionerRole processing)

fields.notes: Often zero as many sites use proprietary codes to store physician specialty.

Organization affiliation from PractitionerRole. This is the location that the provider primarily practices in. fhir-to-omop-demo uses location_ids[0] (line 45). omoponfhir creates a CareSite from the Practitioner's address if no PractitionerRole link exists.

sources.comment: Derived from Practitioner.address via AddressUtil — no PractitionerRole.organization used

entries.notes: Default when specialty system not recognized or code not in OMOP vocabulary

references.notes: Specialty extraction with multi-coding warning (lines 10-23), merge-based approach where PractitionerRole enriches the provider row (lines 31-53), care_site_id from location_ids[0] (line 45), specialty.concept_code -> specialty_source_value (line 49).
All 13 provider fields defined including specialty_concept_id and care_site_id.
Does NOT process PractitionerRole as a separate resource; only the Practitioner resource is mapped. Specialty is not populated. care_site_id is derived from Practitioner.address instead.
No dedicated PractitionerRole mapper. Falls back to Practitioner.qualification[0].code for specialty_source_value.

edge_cases.handling: Single provider row. Use first/most-recent role (by period.end) for specialty and care_site. Log if multiple roles found.
Cannot create or enrich provider row (no identity). Log and skip.

edge_cases.case: Specialty system not recognized (not NUCC, not SNOMED)
PractitionerRole.organization references Organization not in bundle
edge_cases.handling: Deferred resolution. Stub care_site_id; resolve when Organization arrives.
